title: Enhance Tableau PAT Management — Multi-Instance Support and End-User Guidance
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want clear guidance when Tableau content is unavailable, and as an admin, I want to manage multiple Tableau PATs for different sites so users can access the right dashboards.
description:
Comprehensive enhancement to Tableau Personal Access Token (PAT) management:

1. **Multi-instance PAT support**: Users can add PATs for multiple Tableau server/site combinations (e.g., DataTools and Fundcanna). Dashboards are filtered to show only those associated with the selected Tableau site.
2. **Duplicate prevention**: The system prevents adding duplicate tokens for the same server + site name combination.
3. **End-user guidance for edge cases**:
   - No PAT exists and user never connected: redirect to settings with standard setup UX
   - PAT exists but is expired/invalid: show error explaining why and offer option to reconfigure
   - Non-admin user wants Tableau access: two paths — either the admin grants them PAT management rights, or the user sets a personal PAT visible only during their authenticated session
4. **Visibility nuance**: Users with a personal PAT may see Tableau reports they don't have access to inside Tableau itself. This needs documentation.
acceptance_criteria: Multiple Tableau PATs can be added for different server/site combinations
Duplicate server+site PAT entries are prevented
Selecting a Tableau site filters dashboards to that site
Clear error messaging for expired/invalid PATs
Non-admin users have a path to access Tableau content
Blank screen scenario is eliminated with actionable guidance
ux_changes: Added multi-PAT management UI in settings; contextual error/redirect flows for various PAT states; site selector for filtering Tableau dashboards.
middleware_changes: Added logic for multi-PAT storage, duplicate detection, PAT validation, and per-site dashboard filtering.
data_model_changes: Extended PAT storage to support multiple PAT records per account with server and site_name fields.
api_changes: Added endpoints for CRUD operations on multiple Tableau PATs; added site-specific dashboard retrieval.
implementation_notes: Implemented in a separate branch to avoid affecting development. The PAT is set centrally per account. Future consideration: per-user PAT authentication and role-based access control for Tableau metadata.
comment_summary: Waqar implemented multi-PAT support with duplicate prevention and site-specific dashboard filtering. Ryan confirmed the concept was great. No additional testing needed at this time.
